transcript: We’re doing good, LPC. How are you doing? You wanna say, “Hi”?—
Montreal, you wanna say, “Hi” to LPC?

Hi, Montreal! Hi! Hello, oh yeah! Very good. How’s my level? There we go. Okay. Dynamite! [delay, pitch-shifted]I—I’m happy to be here tonight. Thank you, thank you so much! [delay, pitch-shifted] I just had a Beyond Beef burger and that was pretty special! Oh, yeah, uh-huh! [delay, pitch-shifted] So, yeah, oh, yeah!
I’m gettin’ my things together, here. Happy to be with you.

Thanks for this, LPC. Well, people are fairly quiet here. I don’t know if they believe you’re online or not. But, uh… I don’t know, if anybody has any questions for LPC—anything they might wanna ask him—
—you might wanna come up to the phone, so… Don’t line up or anything!

You—you prank called me in your T— at your Toronto show and I was wondering if you had a recording of that and could send it to me. I was the one the one who sounded like the— you said sounded like a cheerleader.

[laughs] [delay, pitch-shifted]Yeah, I totally do! Yeah, Toronto, you said? Um, you should, um, send me a direct message and, uh, then I’ll have your email address and I’ll send it to you. Sure thing, yeah, totally! [delay, pitch-shifted]
And that’ll be super! That’ll be super, superb, and supreme!
Super, superb, and supreme while I’m on da microphone still out fiend—! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Good question! I remember being… at that Toronto show. Wonderful—
[to Vivek ]You should introduce me. [to LPC Hi! Next question.
…has been forwarded to an automatic voice message system.
Hi! I asked your producer if he could sign my record and he denied me, so could you please tell him to do it?
Because I— [breaks up, perhaps, “He did a really good job on the movie] Uh, so like, seven-inch punk records. Like, ’s good for a coaster or frisbee.
So, what are you trying to accomplish?—
Uh, to get your— To get your producer to sign it. For fun—
I dunno! He—he denied me because he said that you’re the real star of the show even though you’re not even here.

Uh, well that’s— That’s awfully, uh, considerate. [coughs loudly] [delay, pitch-shifted]So, um, let’s see, here. Uh… [delay, pitch-shifted]What can we do? We need an arbitrator, apparently, to, uh… weigh in, here. Can we have an audience poll? Who thinks that the producer should sign this kind young man’s, uh, record album? Can we hear from you? Let’s hear ya!
[crowd assents, claps, woman shouts, “Yes! Yes, sign the record!”]
Okay, that’s ok— There we go—
Very quiet, so don’t worry about it.
Okay. [laughs] Oh, boy! [pitch-down]Well, the night’s young. We’ll do somethin’ for ya. I have a sense that’s what’s gonna happen up ‘ere!
Hey, uh, LPC, are you—? Are you good to, uh, to take some calls or some number so that you can call from our audience?
I’m happy to! I’m doing that in the background but no one’s answering, so sure!
Oh, and we’ve got some questions too. But, uh, but yeah, if anybody numbers, wanna come up here and—?
…call has been forwarded to an automatic voice message system.
Or a question. Go for it! Ask your question. We have a question first, LPC.
What is a—? What is a “Sibilant Flutterer”?

Well, it’s, uh— For those of you who don’t know, I have this recording studio here in my house and, uh… I have this rack furniture you can put rack mount equipment in—yada-yada-yada. I’ve got about 40 spaces: a cabinet on the left of the mixer, cabinet on the right of the mixer, and, uh—

Yeah! And there’s a buncha stuff in there. I actually went through and sold some o’ my gear. I mean, I just had tons and tons o’ stuff. But I—I sold some of it, just because. And I didn’t wanna see a blank space in there; it was kinda bummin’ me out, right? So I created my own rack gear that—just a secret between you and I—doesn’t really do anything. But, uh—
I gave them my own names. Like one of ‘em is the “Sibilant Flutterer”. Oh, yeah! Uh-huh, yeah, big time, sure! [delay, pitch-shifted]There’s a “Dissonant Mix Tempest”; that’s another one—
There’s the, uh— This one’s my favorite: “The Lyrical De-Distresser”.

You can switch your vocals between, uh, Rocky, Springsteen, Dylan, Cookie Monster, and so on. And, uh, there’s a few others. What do they do? They just, uh, they put a smile on my face rather than a frown, you know what I mean? [laughs] [delay, pitch-shifted]So, uh, that’s the meaning of this. Thanks for asking. That’s a great question.
Oh, yeah! Hey, thank you! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Yeah, so— So, LPC, we have some numbers.
Okay, what’s the name of this person?—
Oh, their— Their name is Steph.
This is Kip. I’m, uh, new to the area here and I do solar. I do solar panels—

So I’m gonna be, uh, goin’ up on rooftops tomorrow. I just need about 20 minutes on your roof—take some specs, get your measurements—and then, uh, I can go over the details and different quotes for ya on the solar, okay? So I’ll be on your roof—
Okay, well, I don’t— Yeah, I don’t own this house, so I have to make sure the owner of the house is here. So, what time are you gonna come?
Oh, I’ll be there about 4:30 AM—
Yeah, she’ll—sh— Yeah, she’ll be awake at 4:30 but that seems kinda dangerous for you to be goin’ up on my— on the house in the middle of the night. ‘Cause she’s like old—
Well, you know, just between you and I, I take a little, uh, sir or two, uh, calm my nerves kinda thing.
So I’ll be fine. Don’t you worry ‘bout me. I wear gloves and goggles. I get into it. So, uh, you know what I’m sayin’ up there, yeah.
So I’ll be on the roof. Don’t you worry ‘bout a thing. I’m gonna give you a great deal too, okay? A smokin’ deal. ‘Cause I’m new to the area up here! [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
Okay. Ah, well, uh, what’s your name? Kip? Do you have like a business or something?
And can I, like, get, like—? Do you have, like, a website, or…?—
And, uh, I’m gonna give you the best deal I can. And we actually, uh, live—
Wait, tonight at 4:30 or tomorrow at 4? Like, what day at 4:30 in the morning? Like, what day—?
Like, what day, though? Like, I need to know what day.
The seventh; that’ll be tomorrow at 4:30 AM. I’ll be up—

I’ll be on top. Um, if you wanna meet me up there, we can talk about some lunar panels that I’ve got. I’m lettin’ the cat outta the bag slightly. That’s a new development. We’ve got lunar panels comin’ up. Real exciting thoughts—
Okay, yeah, my housemate, she likes that shit. She likes lunar and solar. She’s like an old hippie. So, yeah, I’ll just make sure she’s around. She’ll be awake at 4:30. Okay.
—any kinda pasta or anything—even leftovers—just heat it up; that’d be great for me, okay? ‘Preciate your time.
Thank you so much. Oh, uh-huh! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Hey, I’ve got another number here; you good to go on this one?
[ Wayne Bent sample]It is a defining moment and God’s people are going to be vindicated. [delay, pitch-shifted]
Okay! It’s ringing. What’s this person’s name, please?
—cordia Security. To report an emergency, press “1” now. For all other matters—
—press “2”.[Message repeats in French] Pour signaler une situation d'urgence, appuyez sur le "un". Pour toutes les autres situations, appuyez sur le “deux”.
Concordia Security. How may I help you?
Yeah, I have an opossum here runnin’ wild! It took off with my wristwatch up here!
An opossum did. A wild opossum runnin’ wild! [exhales] What am I supposed to do up here? [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
It’s on top o’ my neighbor’s roof now. It’s got my wristwatch in its teeth, right here! [delay, pitch-shifted] [exhales]
Now, I threw, uh, some walnuts and some almonds at this thing and it’s—it’s not returning my watch—
It’s just not returning my timepiece, here!
Uh, well, I’d—I’d rather not say, at this time.
Okay, no problem! We’ll connect you with 911. Just hold on.
Gonna try Nikki Sixx, here, is what I’m gonna do.
Okay, well, I don’t know if I should hang up or— on these people.

[speaking French]Oui bonjour, on est en ligne avec un monsieur que a appellé de la sécurité Concordia.'[Yes hello, we are on the line with a gentleman who called Concordia Security.]'Il dit qu'il serait sur un des édifices.'[He says he'll be on one of the buildings.]'

Pis ya quelqu'un qui lui a volé sa montre et pis il lance des peanuts sur la personne mais il veut pas confirmer sa localisation.'[Someone stole his watch and he threw peanuts at the person but he doesn't want to confirm his location.]'J'vais vous laisser le monsieur parler.'[I'll let the gentleman talk to you.]'
[speaking French]C'est vous, vous êtes la sécurité Concordia?'[Is that you, Concordia Security?]'

Okay… Pis, la vous nous le passez parce que on— on va être plus en mesure de le localiser dans— dans votre batiment?'[Okay. You passed it to us because we— we will be better able to locate him in— in your building?]'
J’imagine.'[“I imagine”]'[to LPC ] Sir, are you still on the line?
Avez vous le téléphone de où est ce qui appelle?'[Do you have the phone number from where the caller is?]'
Ca aurait été dans les deux-un-trois.
C'est un cellulaire ou c'est un téléphone?'[Is it a cellphone or a landline?]'
[to LPC ] Sir, are you with your cellphone?
Okay. Are you calling from your cellphone?
Pis c'est lui qui vous a appelé?—'[And it was he who called you?—]'
Oh, we got cut off. Oh, weird! Okay, uh…
[laughs] I don’t think I should be tying up 911, anyway, so it’s fine.
It wasn’t 911! It was Concordia Security; they’re very nice, they’re very helpful, they did a very good job, they’re very thorough. Thank you.

Uh, this is Kip. Uh… I’m new here. New— I moved in right around the corner, here. And I—I—I use my whip in the morning. I’m gonna go outside and practice usin’ my horsewhip. I just wanna make sure no one’s gonna be outside.
Just for safety— Aw, fuck! He hung up. Okay. Well—

Nikki Sixx is not to be counted on! Up here. Okay, um… Gallagher. Now, I’ve got a new album coming out this summer and it features Gallagher, um, the comedian. [chuckles] I’m gonna try him again right now, though, too. So… [delay, pitch-shifted] We’ll see how that goes.
[ Wayne Bent samples] Yes— Well— Yes— Well, uh— [delay, pitch-shifted] And nakedness is another symbol of our relationship with God— [delay, pitch-shifted]

This is Kip. Uh… I moved in, uh, ‘round the corner, here. I’m just new to the area and my, uh, buddy gave me your number. I use a big ol’ 28-foot horsewhip and I just wanna make sure you’re not gonna be outside or anything. I need some space out front, there, to use my whip. Just wanna maker sure that’s gonna be okay with you. [exhales] I’m just lookin’ out for ye.
He hung up too. Fuck. Well! [delay, pitch-shifted]
That’s okay too. That is okay. If anyone else has a number, that’d be great.
Yeah, okay, we’re standing by for a number right here. D’you have time for one more, perhaps, uh, one more request and then we’ll, uh, we’ll call it—we’ll call it an evening?
Yeah! I’m gonna change my phone number real quick.
To a different phone numberr… Okay, done. Does anybody have another number? For me?
I’ve got one. Um… this one’s a—
I won’t screw this one up.
[laughs] Oh, shit! I just screwed it up.
Hold on a s— Fuck! What did I do? Ugh! [delay, pitch-shifted]

I—I don’t know. I can’t do it. I fucked it up. I’m sorry. But it’s been wonderful talking to you. I’m sorry, uh, ‘bout that. My thing just, uh, went south. Uh, but Montreal, love you, thank you so much! [delay, pitch-shifted]
And thank you for being here tonight.
Thank you tremendously! Thank you. Thank you.
Thank you. Alright, talk to you soon. Thanks so much—
Thank you. [delay, pitch-shifted] Talk to you soon! Thank you!
[sample of Wayne Bent disciples cheering, shouting, “Liberty!”]
